visits.note: The patient presents with Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, and Hyperlipidemia. HbA1c is 8.1, BP is elevated at 150/95, and LDL is 160. The patient reports symptoms of fatigue and polyuria. The plan is to optimize medications and schedule a follow-up in 4 months.

The patient returns with worsening symptoms of fatigue and polyuria. HbA1c has improved to 7.5, but BP remains elevated at 155/98. LDL has decreased to 140. The patient reports new symptoms of nocturia and erectile dysfunction. The plan is to adjust medications and schedule a follow-up in 4 months.

The patient returns with a 2-month history of worsening fatigue and polyuria. HbA1c has increased to 8.5, and BP is now 160/105. LDL remains elevated at 150. The patient reports new symptoms of paresthesia and numbness in the feet. The plan is to adjust medications and refer to a nephrologist for further evaluation of worsening kidney function.
